title: Change
description: a founder-initiated, primitive-typed unit of work that creates or evolves a Product and the entities beneath it — a prov:Activity (the coarse-grained work-unit sibling of LifecycleRun); ship=commit, nuke=rollback its transaction

valid_from.description: business-truth window start — when this fact became true in the world; null = since-forever

valid_to.description: business-truth window end — when this fact stopped being true; null = still valid

confidence.description: reliability score for this instance; 1.0 = certain, 0 = no confidence

handle.description: the short founder reference (CH-xxxxx) — the human key, distinct from the ULID id (the machine key)

slug.description: kebab-case human name for the change

primitive.description: the change vocabulary — a closed controlled enum (L2 convention), not a separate entity

state.description: the transaction state — ship=commit, nuke=rollback; orthogonal to sys_status (the record-lifecycle axis)

for_product.description: → Product — the product this change contributes to

parent_change.description: → Change (self-edge) — the durable carry link to the change this was spawned from; null = a root change

relationship.description: qualifies parent_change (the ancestry edge); null when parent_change is null

journey.description: → Workflow — the change-lifecycle this change runs (recon→ship), executed by its session; mirrors Scenario.journey (#129 B1)

base_sha.description: git provenance — the commit the change branched from

by_actor.description: → Actor — the founder/agent who ran the change (prov:wasAttributedTo); NOT a login/auth identity

started_at.description: lifecycle timestamp — when the change went in-flight

shipped_at.description: lifecycle timestamp — when the change shipped; null while in-flight (a nuke's end is marked by valid_to, not a separate field)
